summary: Iteration 12 — Manual page + PDF + admin-only RBAC tested end-to-end. Backend 6/6 pytest pass, Frontend admin/viewer flows verified.
critical_code_review_comments: Optional (minor UX): the 'Baixar PDF' button in Manual.js is unconditionally rendered even for non-admin users who hit the route directly. Backend correctly returns 403 and a toast error appears, so functionality is safe. Consider guarding the button and page with a client-side role check for a cleaner UX (currently the empty page + toast is shown briefly before user leaves).

server.py: /api/manual/pdf loads MANUAL.md and rebuilds HTML+PDF on every request. For a static manual, consider caching the generated PDF bytes in memory (invalidate on file mtime) to reduce CPU/latency.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin sidebar nav-manual + /manual page render markdown 8181 chars, 32 headings, 2 tables + PDF download 19KB %PDF; viewer no nav-manual/nav-auditoria + 403 on /manual with error toast, no crash)

test_credentials: Admin admin@fssolucoes.com/admin123 (seeded); viewer@fs.com/viewer123 (idempotently created via /api/auth/register with role=visualizador in test setup).
seed_data_creation: Registered viewer@fs.com (role=visualizador) — persisted user for RBAC tests. No other seed data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Manual feature validated. viewer@fs.com/viewer123 is persisted in DB (visualizador role) and can be reused for future RBAC checks. Backend endpoints: GET /api/manual returns {markdown} for admin (9105+ chars actual, tested >5000), 403 for viewer, 401 unauth. GET /api/manual/pdf returns 19KB application/pdf starting with %PDF for admin, 403 for viewer. Frontend Manual.js uses ReactMarkdown+remarkGfm and axios blob download. Layout.js correctly hides nav-manual and nav-auditoria for non-admin roles (adminOnly flag).
